title: Text segmentation + pronunciation-lexicon tooling
context: Before a chapter can be aligned, the source text must be normalized, segmented into paragraphs and sentences (and optionally words for word-level highlighting), and have its structure preserved (headings, chapters, verse lines, dialogue). Proper nouns, foreign words, archaic spellings, and technical terms must be flagged in a pronunciation lexicon so that they are routed to a skilled reviewer before narration proceeds. This tooling gates alignment quality and learner accuracy for literacy practice. Depends on style-001 (narration style guide defining structure handling and lexicon conventions) and data-001 (per-work record schema).
objective: Implement text-segmentation and pronunciation-lexicon tooling in pipeline/ that normalizes and segments source-text input, preserves document structure, and builds a flagged pronunciation-lexicon per work ready for review.
acceptanceCriteria: Normalizes input text from supported source formats (plain text and basic HTML/XML) and segments it into paragraphs and sentences, with optional word-level tokenization.
Preserves document structure: headings, chapter boundaries, verse lines, and dialogue markers are retained and emitted in the segment output.

Automatically flags candidate lexicon items for pronunciation review: proper nouns (capitalized tokens in mid-sentence), hyphenated or archaic forms, words matching a foreign-language pattern or technical-term list, and tokens flagged by a configurable rule.
Produces a pronunciation-lexicon file per work in the format defined by data-001 (term, IPA/respelling placeholder, reviewedBy); flags items as needs-review until a reviewer clears them.
Integrates with the per-work record schema (data-001) to write the pronunciationLexicon array and segment metadata.
Passes pnpm build and pnpm test (unit tests covering: paragraph/sentence segmentation, structure preservation, proper-noun flagging, lexicon output format); pnpm lint clean.
Licensed MIT; DCO sign-off on all commits.
resources: read-aloud-audio-style-001 (narration style guide: structure handling, lexicon conventions, item classes to flag)
PLAN.md (prepare & segment text step, pronunciation lexicon component)
output: A merged PR adding pipeline/segment-and-lexicon/ with the text-segmentation and pronunciation-lexicon tooling, unit tests, and documentation covering supported input formats, structure-preservation rules, flagging heuristics, and the lexicon output format.
